---
title: "Literature Review"
---

## Research Landscape

Organise related work into 2–4 thematic clusters. Each cluster follows the same
**What → Why → How** structure: *what* and *why* are one-sentence scan-points;
*how* details are hidden behind a disclosure toggle and prefaced by a diagram.

```{dot}
//| label: fig-landscape
//| fig-cap: "Literature landscape: foundations → clusters → this work"
//| fig-width: 7
//| fig-height: 3.5
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=TB
  graph [ranksep=0.55, nodesep=0.5]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.6, height=0.42, margin="0.15,0.08"]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph row1 {
    rank=same
    F1 [label="Cluster A", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    F2 [label="Cluster B", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    F3 [label="Cluster C", fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  }
  subgraph row2 {
    rank=same
    G1 [label="Gap A", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
    G2 [label="Gap B", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
    G3 [label="Gap C", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
  }
  subgraph row3 {
    rank=same
    Us [label="This Work", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c", width=5.0]
  }

  F1 -> G1  F2 -> G2  F3 -> G3
  G1 -> Us  G2 -> Us  G3 -> Us
}
```

---

## Cluster A — [Category Name, e.g., "Deep Feature Extractors"]

- **What**: [One sentence — what this line of work produces or achieves.]
- **Why it emerged**: [One sentence — what open problem or limitation drove its creation.]
- **Why it's insufficient**: [One sentence — the concrete gap that this work addresses.]

> Key papers: [@ref1], [@ref2], [@ref3]

```{dot}
//| label: fig-cluster-a
//| fig-cap: "Cluster A: core mechanism"
//| fig-width: 7
//| fig-height: 2.8
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=LR
  graph [ranksep=0.6, nodesep=0.45]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.55, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph col1 {
    rank=same
    In1 [label="Input A",  fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    In2 [label="Input B",  fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  }
  subgraph col2 {
    rank=same
    Op1 [label="Op 1"]
    Op2 [label="Op 2"]
  }
  subgraph col3 {
    rank=same
    Out [label="Output", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
    Gap [label="Gap",    fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
  }

  In1 -> Op1  In2 -> Op2
  Op1 -> Out  Op2 -> Out
  Out -> Gap [style=dashed, color="#e74c3c"]
}
```

<details>
<summary><strong>How — Core Ideas and Steps</strong></summary>

**Core idea**: [one sentence — the key insight or inductive bias]

Steps:

1. [Step 1 — input processing or encoding]
2. [Step 2 — main transformation]
3. [Step 3 — output or prediction]

Key design choices:

- [Choice A]: [why it was made; alternative considered]
- [Choice B]: [why it was made; alternative considered]

Limitation in detail: [explain precisely why the gap arises from the mechanism, not just the result]

</details>

---

## Cluster B — [Category Name, e.g., "Regularisation Methods"]

- **What**: [One sentence — what this line of work produces or achieves.]
- **Why it emerged**: [One sentence — what open problem or limitation drove its creation.]
- **Why it's insufficient**: [One sentence — the concrete gap that this work addresses.]

> Key papers: [@ref4], [@ref5]

```{dot}
//| label: fig-cluster-b
//| fig-cap: "Cluster B: core mechanism"
//| fig-width: 7
//| fig-height: 2.8
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=LR
  graph [ranksep=0.6, nodesep=0.45]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.55, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph col1 {
    rank=same
    In1 [label="Input A",  fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    In2 [label="Input B",  fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  }
  subgraph col2 {
    rank=same
    Op1 [label="Op 1"]
    Op2 [label="Op 2"]
  }
  subgraph col3 {
    rank=same
    Out [label="Output", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
    Gap [label="Gap",    fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
  }

  In1 -> Op1  In2 -> Op2
  Op1 -> Out  Op2 -> Out
  Out -> Gap [style=dashed, color="#e74c3c"]
}
```

<details>
<summary><strong>How — Core Ideas and Steps</strong></summary>

**Core idea**: [one sentence — the key insight or inductive bias]

Steps:

1. [Step 1 — input processing or encoding]
2. [Step 2 — main transformation]
3. [Step 3 — output or prediction]

Key design choices:

- [Choice A]: [why it was made; alternative considered]
- [Choice B]: [why it was made; alternative considered]

Limitation in detail: [explain precisely why the gap arises from the mechanism]

</details>

---

## Cluster C — [Category Name, e.g., "Scalable Training Strategies"]

- **What**: [One sentence — what this line of work produces or achieves.]
- **Why it emerged**: [One sentence — what open problem or limitation drove its creation.]
- **Why it's insufficient**: [One sentence — the concrete gap that this work addresses.]

> Key papers: [@ref6], [@ref7], [@ref8]

```{dot}
//| label: fig-cluster-c
//| fig-cap: "Cluster C: core mechanism"
//| fig-width: 7
//| fig-height: 2.8
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=LR
  graph [ranksep=0.6, nodesep=0.45]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.55, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph col1 {
    rank=same
    In1 [label="Input A",  fillcolor="#375a7f"]
    In2 [label="Input B",  fillcolor="#375a7f"]
  }
  subgraph col2 {
    rank=same
    Op1 [label="Op 1"]
    Op2 [label="Op 2"]
  }
  subgraph col3 {
    rank=same
    Out [label="Output", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
    Gap [label="Gap",    fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
  }

  In1 -> Op1  In2 -> Op2
  Op1 -> Out  Op2 -> Out
  Out -> Gap [style=dashed, color="#e74c3c"]
}
```

<details>
<summary><strong>How — Core Ideas and Steps</strong></summary>

**Core idea**: [one sentence — the key insight or inductive bias]

Steps:

1. [Step 1 — input processing or encoding]
2. [Step 2 — main transformation]
3. [Step 3 — output or prediction]

Key design choices:

- [Choice A]: [why it was made; alternative considered]
- [Choice B]: [why it was made; alternative considered]

Limitation in detail: [explain precisely why the gap arises from the mechanism]

</details>

---

## Gap Synthesis

From the three clusters above, three concrete gaps converge to motivate this work:

- **Gap A** (from Cluster A): [one sentence — what specifically is missing]
- **Gap B** (from Cluster B): [one sentence — what specifically is missing]
- **Gap C** (from Cluster C): [one sentence — what specifically is missing]

```{dot}
//| label: fig-gap-synthesis
//| fig-cap: "Three gaps → this work's contributions"
//| fig-width: 7
//| fig-height: 2.8
digraph G {
  bgcolor="transparent"
  rankdir=LR
  graph [ranksep=0.6, nodesep=0.45]
  node [style=filled, fillcolor="#2c3035", color="#4a9eda",
        fontcolor="#f8f9fa", fontname="Arial", fontsize=11,
        shape=box, width=1.55, height=0.42]
  edge [color="#6c757d", penwidth=1.4]

  subgraph col1 {
    rank=same
    GA [label="Gap A", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
    GB [label="Gap B", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
    GC [label="Gap C", fillcolor="#4a2040", color="#e74c3c"]
  }
  subgraph col2 {
    rank=same
    C1 [label="Contrib 1", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
    C2 [label="Contrib 2", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
    C3 [label="Contrib 3", fillcolor="#1a4a3a", color="#00bc8c"]
  }

  GA -> C1  GB -> C2  GC -> C3
}
```

<details>
<summary><strong>How — Positioning Argument</strong></summary>

**vs. Cluster A best method** ([@ref2]): same problem formulation; this work improves by [mechanism X] — directly addresses Gap A.

**vs. Cluster B best method** ([@ref5]): different scope; this work generalises to [scenario] that [@ref5] excludes.

**vs. Cluster C best method** ([@ref8]): concurrent work; key difference is [design decision Y] — empirically validated in [experiment].

</details>
